q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: The sink is text(f"text like '%{search_param}%'") on line 33, which constructs a SQL text clause object but does not perform DB I/O itself.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The function returns render_template(...); the SQL text clause with the injected string is not part of the return value.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' but the return expression is a rendered template, not the SQL text object.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the text() call on line 33 (e.g., from sqlalchemy) to intercept the SQL string being constructed.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full f-string argument passed to text(), specifically the interpolated search_param value, to verify that user input is embedded unsanitized in the SQL fragment.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a lightweight dummy or pass-through object that satisfies downstream query building without triggering actual database execution.
notes: Also mock any subsequent session.execute() or equivalent DB call to prevent real I/O if the query is executed within the same function or immediately after.
The core assertion should check that the captured SQL string contains the raw user input rather than a parameterized placeholder.

warnings: Q1 is borderline: text() itself is safe, but the broader SQL execution pipeline is dangerous; if the actual DB execution is considered part of the sink, this could be reclassified.
